calendar.matters:
MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Oct-28-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT XING DU'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND.

No appearances are made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Du's motion to vacate the default judgment entered on May 11, 2021 is denied. First, Du does not seek to vacate the underlying default, which was entered on July 28, 2020, so vacating only the default judgment serves little purpose. (See Howard Greer Custom Originals v. Capritti (1950) 35 Cal.2d 886, 889 ["The setting aside and vacating the judgment alone, which was all the relief sought in said motion, would have been an idle act, because the default . . . would have stood undisturbed. . . . . If the judgment were vacated it would be the duty of the court immediately to render another judgment of like effect, and the defendants, still being in default, could not be heard in opposition thereto" (citations omitted)].) Second, defendant is the President and incorporator of co-defendant Hesti Cabinet Co. Inc. (Adam Decl., Exs. 1-3.) Immediately after service of the complaint, Hesti and plaintiff communicated regarding the lawsuit, with Hesti acknowledging that it had received the summons and complaint and asking plaintiff whether it could resolve the matter and disregard the complaint or "we must proceed with this court?" (Fitzgerald Decl., Ex. 1.) Given defendant's role at Hesti, defendant's assertion that he was unaware of the lawsuit lacks credibility. In addition, defendant does not explain what prompted him to consult counsel and exactly how or when he discovered the lawsuit. (Du Decl.) Lastly, defendant fails to show that he brought this motion within a reasonable time. (See Code Civ. Proc. sec. 473.5(a).) The request to enter default was served on defendant on July 28, 2020 and the notice of entry of judgment was served on May 19, 2021. Defendant, however, waited until September 24, 2021 to file this motion, and offers no explanation for the delay. (See Stafford v. Mach (1998) 64 Cal.App.4th 1174, 1184 ["delays of three months or more routinely result in denial of relief where there is no satisfactory explanation for the delay."].)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)

Motion For Default Judgment And Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion ;
Off Calendar Nov-10-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants Xing Lun Du and Hesti Cabinet Co. Inc.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gerardo C. Sandoval. Clerk: Lyssette Bareng. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Amanda Ferns, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Pawnee Leasing Corporation.
Kenny Fitzgerald, Vice President of Legal and Asset Management for Pawnee Leasing Corporation, was placed under oath and testified.
Both counsel and witness for plaintiff appeared via ZOOM Video Conference.
No appearance by defendants Xing Lun Du and Hesti Cabinet Co. Inc.
Plaintiff's Complaint for Breach of Lease, Breach of Guaranty and Possession of Private Property was filed on April 27, 2020.
Substitute service of plaintiff's Summons, Complaint and accompanying documents on defendant Xing Lun Du occurred on May 27, 2020, and was effectuated on June 7, 2020.
Substitute service of plaintiff's Summons, Complaint and accompanying documents on defendant Hesti Cabinet Co. Inc., occurred on May 19, 2020, and was effectuated on May 29, 2020.
Default as to both defendants was entered on July 28, 2020.
DOE defendants were dismissed without prejudice on July 28, 2020.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Pawnee Leasing Corporation, and against the defaulted defendants Xing Lun Du and Hesti Cabinet Co. Inc., Jointly and Severally, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $32,583.90.
Prejudgment Interest - $1,186.36.
Attorney Fees - $4,355.00.
Costs - $590.00.

Total - $38,715.26.

In addition, the Court GRANTED possession of the 2 units of Belfab NBM-09 15 H.P. Dust Collection Systems with Baghouse/Cyclone/Hopper/Piping.

Counsel for plaintiffs shall prepare the proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.
